hero.question: Chicago Cubs vs. Cleveland Guardians
hero.prob_take: At 76.5%, the market is pricing the Cubs as a heavy favorite, but that 25.5-point surge in 24 hours screams panic buying rather than genuine edge. Cleveland's bats have shown life in recent matchups and the Guardians' pitching stays underrated by casual bettors who chase recency bias on big moves.

daily_take.headline: Cubs moneyline just jumped 25 points. Here's what sharp money sees.
daily_take.deck: The Cubs just went from 51% to 76.5% in 24 hours on $228K volume. This isn't random noise. Either new information hit the market (injury news, weather data, line movement from sportsbooks), or the market was badly mispriced and smart money pounced. Check injury reports first. If the Guardians lost a starter or the Cubs got surprise good news, the move makes sense. If nothing changed, you're looking at either a soft market that got hammered by one large bettor or genuine sharp consensus breaking toward Chicago. The play: don't chase the move. Wait for the line to stabilize and cross-reference with traditional sportsbooks to see if they've adjusted similarly. If they haven't, this could be arbitrage or a sucker pump.
